# Running Helper Scripts Manually

As developers, you will frequently use the helper scripts located in `assets/scripts/` to rapidly generate test data, build manifests, and validate the wrangling logic.

For absolute conceptual knowledge or user-facing arguments of these scripts, refer to the [Additional Utilities](../guide/additional_utilities.qmd) manual.

First, ensure your [Environment](python_environment.qmd) is properly set up.

---

## Generating Synthetic Test Data

To inject edge cases (duplicates, mismatches, and nulls) for testing the ingestion layer locally:

```bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$ROOT/assets/scripts"
DATA_IN_DIR="assets/test_data/raw"

python3 $SCRIPT_DIR/create_test_data.py \
  --data_file $DATA_IN_DIR/raw_pipeline_output.tsv \
  --primary_key_data sample_id \
  --mismatches --duplicates --missing_values 
```

---

## Auto-Scaffolding a Modular Manifest

To test the Native Modular YAML scaffolding (`!include`), point the manifest generator at a directory of extracted TSV files. It will automatically build the `Master File` and a folder containing the separate schema fragments.

```bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$ROOT/assets/scripts"
DUMMYDATA_DIR="assets/test_data/my_extracted_pipeline"

python3 $SCRIPT_DIR/create_manifest.py \
  --data_dir $DUMMYDATA_DIR \
  --primary_key_data sample_id \
  --out_file config/manifests/pipelines/My_New_Test.yaml
```

*(Remember to manually align the generated `target_dataset` array inside the Plotly config block after generation!)*
